methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
FlyBy Robotics is a seed-stage defense and security robotics company that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 10 peers across public and private markets—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI, spanning seed through Series A stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $12M. The peer-median method examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Tenna Systems at $60M (seed), Ares Industries at $150M (Series A), Vatn Systems at $300M (Series A), and Stark Defence at $500M (Series A). The median across this set is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $11M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $119M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around FlyBy's relative competitive position and path to scale within a heterogeneous peer set spanning two stages and a 8.3x valuation spread. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to observable capital; the peer-median method reflects market multiples but introduces ambiguity given the significant stage and valuation heterogeneity among comps. Validation would benefit from tighter stage-aligned peer filtering and clarity on FlyBy's product differentiation and customer traction relative to the seed-stage comparable.
